observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain complete subsidiary records and financial statements, using the prescribed sub-codes for all the special accounts operated by the LGU and to submit copies of the same to the Management to provide them of the vital information to  effectively manage and implement the different programs/projects and activities of these special accounts.

Such records and financial statements shall also be submitted and made available to this Office to facilitate our review/audit.
observations.managementAction:
Conducted partial discussion with the EPAC system provider in 2023 as it  needs budget and appropriation figures.

The supplemental budget was approved in 2024 for the system procurement is already approved and to undergo procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.  Direct the Inventory Committee / Appraisal Committee to fast track the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Accounting Unit to serve as basis in the recording of Impairment Loss; and 

b.Create and require the Disposal Committee to refer to the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 89-296 for the disposal of properties.

We recommended that Management:

1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;

2.Direct the Budget Officer to include the subject payables in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and

3.With the current ease of COVID-19 restrictions, maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of leave benefits payable.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is dependent only on the available income for the budget, priority projects were given priority appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor request assistance of the Barangays to prepare an inventory of electric posts within their area so that the  Municipal Assessor will be able to properly assess and prepare the tax declaration of MORESCO’s electric posts to enable the Province to bill  and collect the correct amount of real property taxes thereon.
observations.managementAction:
The Management requested for an ANC meeting to discuss this matter and eventually for immediate action.

Inventory of MORESCO electric post were submitted to the provincial assessor's office for tax declaration application. 

Currently pending for follow-up with same office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office to communicate with the system’s developer for system reprogramming that would enable the Municipality to classify consumers and apply the correct water rates in accordance with its Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The MEEDO is currently classifying consumers to apply the correct water rates in billing through the LGU’s billing system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts, make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements, and furnish COA with the corrections made therein.

We recommended and management concurred to:
1.   Require the Municipal Accountant (MA) and GSO-Designate to reconcile the discrepancies between the PPE accounts recorded in the books, and that appearing in the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustment/s to come up with the correct balances and
2.  Direct the Property Unit to reconcile the results of inventory with the property records on file together with the PAR  and ICS issued to end-users using the RPCPPE as basis, and discrepancies should be verified and adjusted/corrected accordingly.

We recommended and management agreed to regularly prepare and submit monthly reports for Official Travel and Fuel Consumption within the prescribed period to afford the audit team ample time to review them.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the submission of complete supporting documents prior to approval of payments to ensure that only those with legal and valid claims are paid.

We recommended and management concurred to the conduct of a study to determine the optimal number of employees under the MTO to address volumetric duties assigned to limited number of employees. Management should also enforce daily remittance by the Collector to the Municipal Treasurer to avoid possible loss, misuse, and misappropriation of government funds and the proper recognition of revenue when collected.

We recommended and management agreed to submit the DVs for November and December 2023 immediately and regularly prepare and promptly submit monthly reports within the prescribed period.  Also, management should ensure that complete documentation be secured before payment so that only the act of filing at month-end can be undertaken.

We recommended and management is amenable to update the Revenue Code following RA No. 7160 and DOF-DILG JMC No. 2019-01 through the issuance of a Municipal Tax Ordinance and undertake the required public consultation and information dissemination for approval by the Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan to improve its revenue generation to fund its much-needed programs, projects, and activities.

We recommended and management agreed to immediately submit FURs for the remaining unliquidated fund transfers for SYs 2020-2021 and 2021-2022. Thereafter, fast-track the submission of the next batch of FURs covering the SY 2022-2023.  Once submitted, prepare appropriate journal entry voucher to record the liquidation/s thereof.

We recommended and management concurred to strictly adhere to the requirements of this Circular.  Henceforth, at the beginning of the year, cause the submission of the list of all ongoing government PPAs and those to be implemented during the year.  Further, submit within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project award or before the program/activity starts the relevant report/information that the appropriate signboards and public notices are already posted. Lastly, submit the lacking quarterly reports on government PPAs for CY 2023.

We recommended and Management concurred to monitor and expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% LDF to avoid delays in adequately delivering the essential services and maximize the socio-economic benefits intended for its constituents. Convene the Local Development Council to consider revisiting the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and the 20% Local Development Fund Plan to ascertain and prioritize the implementation of feasible socio-economic programs, projects, and activities that will benefit the general welfare of its constituents in conformity with the provisions of Section 4 of DBM-DOF-DILG Circular No. 2020-01 and RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management instruct the personnel preparing the PO to accomplish and provide all necessary information/ terms in the Purchase Orders to provide the necessary basis in determining the  obligation of the Suppliers and the regularity of the Pos/ procurement process as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 and the Manual NGAS for LGUs.

Likewise, enjoin the Municipal Accounting  Office to submit within five days from perfection, copies of the purchase orders/contracts, including the  supporting documents, to this Office, to facilitate timely review of the terms and reasonableness of the costs involved and enable this Office to communicate timely to management any deficiencies that may be uncovered for proper action.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned official from the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and or the specific focal person tasked for this purpose, to submit copies of all perfected contracts and purchase orders, together with the complete supporting documents, to this office within five days from its perfection in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct: 

1. The Municipal Treasurer to direct all Revenue Collectors to remit all their collections promptly and to review all their Report of Collections and Deposits to ensure that all collections and issued and unissued accountable forms are fully accounted to prevent loss or misuse of collections. The Treasurer shall:

a.Verify the Report of Collections and Deposits particularly to examine the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; 

b.Conduct a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collector and check the same against new balances on hand column; and 
Accountable forms shall be issued only to those  accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I; and 

2.  All Revenue Collectors to prepare their respective RAAF and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Consolidated RAAF and to submit these to our Office within the 5th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain the necessary subsidiary ledgers of all revenue collectors s to establish their accountability at any time and substantiate and reconciled with the GL balance.

observations.managementAction: Old aged accounts receivables from officers & employees, disbursing officers were collected and paid except previous employees (2) Allan E. Acain and Alice Balido who were already separated from service.

Municipal Assessor has not updated their list of true and real declarant of the property. 

Problem when the property is being sold and the new owner failed to transfer the same thus the Treasurer don’t know where to collect real property tax.

An ongoing identification of the real declarant of the real properties are now made with the assistance of Barangay Officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the
Municipal Accountant to:
1.Orient the newly designated AOs as to their duties and responsibilities as SDOs especially on the proper recording of pertinent transactions, and ensure immediate briefing of all new AOs thereafter;
2. Give a refresher to existing AOs on the proper record-keeping in their cashbooks.

We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the General Services Officer-Designate, strictly observe the proper management and accounting of supplies and inventory in accordance with  Sections 114, 115 and 124 of the NGAS Manual by:

1.Recognizing an inventory account upon delivery of procured supplies and materials (Section 114);

2.Maintaining supply/materials stock card and ledger cards under the perpetual inventory method (Section 115); and

3. Creating an inventory committee who will be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory (Section 124).
observations.managementAction:
The Management has not provided a storage room in the new Municipal Hall Building. 

Concerned offices maintained supply/materials stock cards and ledger cards recognized as inventory accounts in books based on actual stock per office.

The inventory committee was not yet created to conduct the physical counts and submit reports to the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

1. Provide adequate budget for the enhancement of the ECPAC accounting system that is capable of preparation/ generation of the following reports:

a. The Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) in accordance with Section 18 of the NGAS Manual;

b. The Statement of Comparison of Budgeted and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) that can be verified based on supporting documents and records in compliance to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS No. 1 and Section 111 of PD 1445 to ensure that the budgeted and  actual amounts of income, expenditure and capital outlay accounts reported in the SCBAA are fairly presented.

2.Require the Municipal Budget Office to maintain accurate subsidiary records in posting the actual obligations by using the details of the Disbursement Voucher as supporting documents instead of the Purchase Request which may not be actualized or realized as an expenditure or obligation.
observations.managementAction:
The system technician was unable to visit and demonstrate the ECPAC accounting system due to COVID-19 restrictions.

Management has already asked the system provider to make a proposed quotation to enhance the system that will cater to the needed reports. The supplemental budget for 2024 for the system procurement is already approved and to undergo procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to ensure preparation of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks, a list of checks which are unreleased or undelivered at the end of the year, and submission thereof to the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant to refer to the Schedule of Unreleased Checks for the preparation of the adjusting entry to restore the value of the unreleased checks to the cash account balance and set-up the appropriate liability, and make an entry the following year to reverse such adjustments.  Also, regular coordination with the Treasurer and vice versa relative to this matter and all other concerns of the agency involving the Accounting and Treasury Offices is highly encouraged.

We recommended that Management require the Accountant to:
Exert reasonable efforts to determine the details of the accounts receivable and to retrieve pertinent documents;
Assess/evaluate the condition of any identified dormant receivables in order to determine whether which are still recoverable and/or which are subject to possible write-off.
For receivables determined to be recoverable, exhaust all possible remedies to enforce collection thereof.
For receivables determined to be beyond recovery in consonance with COA Circular No. 2016-005, facilitate the filing of request for authority to write-off following the guidelines and procedures set forth in the Circular.  Also, recognize impairment for accounts no longer collectible pursuant to the aforementioned accounting standards.
Ensure documentation, regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure prompt collection thereof when these become due and demandable, and to prevent the accumulation of dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee / Appraisal Committee to fast track the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Accounting Unit to serve as basis in the recording of Impairment Loss; and
Create and require the Disposal Committee to refer to the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 89-296 for the disposal of properties.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;
Direct the Budget Officer to include the subject payables in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and
With the current ease of COVID-19 restrictions, maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of leave benefits payable.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant or the Municipal Treasurer to request from the creditor-banks a copy of the respective amortization schedules of all loans payable of the Municipality; and
Based on the amortization schedule, require the Municipal Accountant to classify the portion of the loans which is payable in the succeeding year or within 12 months from the reporting date under the Current Liabilities section of the SFPos.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to properly monitor unliquidated cash advances and ensure that all cash advances of SDOs are fully liquidated and any unexpended balance refunded at year-end and not used to pay for expenses in the succeeding year.  To avoid discontinuity of any activity which cut across two fiscal years, refer to Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 for the grant of new cash advances at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to enforce among SDOs and/or others concerned strict implementation of the liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period and make it a prerequisite in the grant of new cash advance; and
To avoid interrupted operations, instruct the SDOs to record daily transactions immediately and chronologically/systematically sort and file supporting documents every time to facilitate prompt liquidation of cash advances and immediate drawing of new cash advance.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure strict observance of the timely release of the barangays’ share in the Municipality’s collection of RPT during the quarter by being conscious and reminding concerned personnel within their respective offices of the timelines for the processing of pertinent transactions pursuant to Section 271(d), Chapter 7, Title II, Book II of the Local Government Code; and
In coordination with the Personnel Unit, review and evaluate the manning of the Treasury and Accounting Office and consider detailing or adding more full-time / permanent staff to assist the existing personnel therein.

We recommended that Management require the following:
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Review/monitor regularly the transactions recorded in the cashbook/s to ensure that errors, unrecorded transactions and other necessary adjustments be promptly corrected and effected in the cashbook/s;
Make written aside from verbal follow-ups with LBP on the approval of the request for access to “We-Access”; and
Request for snapshots from the bank at the end of each month and forward them to the Accountant to be used as basis in the preparation of the BRS while still waiting for the approval of the above request.
The Municipal Accountant to make use of the bank snapshots in the preparation of the BRS in the meantime that We-Access is still not yet available, and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in informing and requesting the bank to possibly furnish them with a clearer or more legible copies of the snapshots; and
Both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that their respective records on cash be regularly reconciled, and detected errors and unrecorded transactions be promptly adjusted therein.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Orient the newly designated AOs as to their duties and responsibilities as SDOs especially on the proper recording of pertinent transactions, and ensure immediate briefing of all new AOs thereafter; and
Give a refresher to existing AOs on the proper record-keeping in their cashbooks.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD focal person to ensure that programs/projects/activities formulated and included in the GPB and subsequently undertaken and charged against the GAD budget are in line with the purpose for which GAD was created which, above all, should address gender issues in adherence to the requirements of PCW-NEDA-DILG JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that Management:

	Fast track the conduct of the physical count of PPE and the submission of RPCPPE pursuant to the guidelines prescribed in the aforementioned COA circular; 

	Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property cards, and ensure regular reconciliation thereof and other related records; 

	Instruct the Property Division/Unit to determine the person/s accountable for the non-existing/missing PPEs based on the Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs), Property Cards (PCs) and other available property records, and thereafter issue demand letters to produce the missing PPE; 

Direct the Inventory Committee, the Property and the Accounting Divisions/Units to refer to Sections 7 to 9 of the same COA circular for the procedures in the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs; and

	Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately take up the following adjustments in the books and ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of the GAM thereafter:

Adjust the carrying value and recognize the depreciation expenses for the prior years 
Prior Years’ Adjustments    	xx	
	Accumulated Depreciation	  xx

To adjust PPE carrying value and take up prior year’s depreciation expense/s

	Provide for depreciation expense of the PPE for the current year
Depreciation Expense    xx	
	Accumulated Depreciation   		xx

To adjust PPE carrying value and take up prior year’s depreciation expense
observations.reason:
Ongoing update of PPE Ledger Cards and PPE Cards

Procedures in the derecognition of PPE were not properly taken

Reiterated with modification under Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.	 Require the Municipal Accountant to appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;

b.	 Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables accumulated from prior years up to the present; and

c.	 Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of leave benefits payable.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification under Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe diligence in the utilization of the LDRRM Fund and refrain from including in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and charging to LDRRMF expenditures which are not related or directly attributable to disaster risk management activities in order to preserve sufficient funds for later use and for its intended purpose pursuant to the aforementioned COA circular.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure at all times that the Performance Securities posted by winning bidders prior to the execution of the contracts are sufficient in amount and proper in form in accordance with the aforementioned provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to:

a. 	Immediately submit proof of approval of the CSHP by DOLE for the aforementioned projects; and

b.	 Though the current practice of requiring the winning bidder to submit only the duly received requests for approval of CSHP by DOLE may be allowed considering the time constraints for the submission of contracts to COA, ensure thereafter that the contractor eventually submits a copy of the CSHP of the infrastructure projects which is already approved by the DOLE.

We recommended that management instruct the concerned personnel/unit to:

1.	 Fast track the disposal of the obsolete unused checks in compliance with the procedures provided in the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations; and

2.	 Ensure thereafter that all unserviceable/obsolete properties/items are properly accounted, documented and disposed accordingly.

We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the General Services Officer-Designate, strictly observe the proper management and accounting of supplies and inventory in accordance with Sections 114, 115 and 124 of the NGAS Manual by:

 Recognizing an inventory account upon delivery of procured supplies and materials (Section 114);

 Maintaining supply/materials stock card and ledger cards under the perpetual inventory method (Section 115); and

 Creating an inventory committee who will be responsible in the conduct of physical count of supplies every semester, and furnish reports to Accounting Office for the accrual or recognition of expense and proper valuation of the unused office supplies which will be considered as inventory (Section 124).

Failed to observe proper inventory and management of supplies. 

As the municipality have procured supplies in least quantity that can be consumed during the report year, thus zero supplies inventory at the report period.

There has been a creation of an inventory committee thru an executive order. No report has been furnished to the accounting office yet.

The application for the “We Access” was still not approved.

The	Bank Statement from LBP was delayed by an average of 2-3 months.

Delay	in	the furnishing of bank credit/debit memos	by	the bank

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Accountant- Designate and the General Services Officer-Designate, strictly observe the proper management and accounting of supplies and inventory in accordance with Sections 114, 115 and 124 of the NGAS Manual by:

1. Recognizing an inventory   account upon delivery of procured supplies and materials (Section 114);

Maintaining supply/materials stock card and ledger cards under the perpetual inventory method (Section 115); and

3. Creating an inventory committee who will be responsible in the conduct of physical count of supplies every semester, and furnish reports to Accounting Office for the accrual or recognition of expense and proper valuation of the unused office supplies which will be considered as inventory (Section 124).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and/ or the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor a Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 to determine the propriety in the disbursements of the Fund.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the DRMM Funds from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management strictly observe Administrative Order No. 17 s. 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to comply with the requirements of the DBM-PS in the preparation and submission of the APP-CSE to ensure inclusion of the Municipality’s inventory requirements in the government-wide projected common-use supplies and equipment procurement in the fiscal year.

observations.recommendation:

1. Provide adequate budget for the enhancement of the ECPAC accounting system that is capable of preparation/ generation of the following reports:

The Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) in accordance with Section 18 of the NGAS Manual;

The Statement of Comparison of Budgeted and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) that can be verified based on supporting documents and records in compliance to Paragraph 27 of PPSAS No. 1 and Section 111 of PD 1445 to ensure that the budgeted and actual amounts of income, expenditure and capital outlay accounts reported in the SCBAA are fairly presented.

2. Require the Municipal Budget Office to maintain accurate subsidiary records in posting the actual obligations by using the details of the Disbursement Voucher as supporting documents instead of the Purchase Request which may not be actualized or realized as an expenditure or obligation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the DRMM Funds from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management strictly observe Administrative Order No. 17 s. 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to comply with the requirements of the DBM-PS in the preparation and submission of the APP-CSE to ensure inclusion of the Municipality’s inventory requirements in the government-wide projected common-use supplies and equipment procurement in the fiscal year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Head of the Procuring Entity (HoPE), require the BAC to strictly comply with Annex G of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184, particularly in the preparation and submission of preliminary design and construction studies, duly approved by the HoPE, prior to the bidding of the projects.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to:
Revisit Rule XVII of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 on how to handle protest or request for reconsideration filed by the bidders; and
Ensure that interested bidders were notified of any change in the schedule of opening of bids.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Ensure that the bid evaluation was conducted thoroughly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Strictly observe the “pass/fail” criteria in evaluating the eligibility of the bidders against the requirements in the Bidding Documents and the RA 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Require the contractor to post additional Performance Security before the expiry of the submitted performance bond covering the liability period up to the date of final acceptance of the project; and
Ensure that performance security posted by the winning bidder conforms to the prescribed type, form and wording, amount and validity period as required under Section 39 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management ensure that the projects implemented by administration conform to the quality standards for farm-to-market roads and ensure that effectiveness, efficiency and economy in the implementation of its projects are observed to prevent loss or wastage of the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that Management ensure that the evacuation centers to be constructed conform to the design requirements prescribed in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 122 s. 2018 responsive to the needs of the intended users.

We recommended that the Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor request assistance of the Barangays to prepare an inventory of electric posts within their area so that the Municipal Assessor will be able to properly assess and prepare the tax declaration of MORESCO’s electric posts to enable the Province to bill  and collect the correct amount of real property taxes thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Head of the Procuring Entity (HoPE), require the BAC to strictly comply with Annex G of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA 9184, particularly in the preparation and submission of preliminary design and construction studies, duly approved by the HoPE, prior to the bidding of the projects.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to:
Revisit Rule XVII of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 on how to handle protest or request for reconsideration filed by the bidders; and
Ensure that interested bidders were notified of any change in the schedule of opening of bids.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Ensure that the bid evaluation was conducted thoroughly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Strictly observe the “pass/fail” criteria in evaluating the eligibility of the bidders against the requirements in the Bidding Documents and the RA 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Require the contractor to post additional Performance Security before the expiry of the submitted performance bond covering the liability period up to the date of final acceptance of the project; and
Ensure that performance security posted by the winning bidder conforms to the prescribed type, form and wording, amount and validity period as required under Section 39 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management ensure that the projects implemented by administration conform to the quality standards for farm-to-market roads and ensure that effectiveness, efficiency and economy in the implementation of its projects are observed to prevent loss or wastage of the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that Management ensure that the evacuation centers to be constructed conform to the design requirements prescribed in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 122 s. 2018 responsive to the needs of the intended users.

We recommended that the Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor request assistance of the Barangays to prepare an inventory of electric posts within their area so that the Municipal Assessor will be able to properly assess and prepare the tax declaration of MORESCO’s electric posts to enable the Province to bill  and collect the correct amount of real property taxes thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe COA Circular No. 96-11 by prioritizing the preparation and the timely submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements to the COA Auditor and to enroll in the on-line facility of the Banks for viewing and printing access of bank statements to facilitate preparation thereof.
observations.reason:
BRS for July to November 2019 submitted to COA on February 21, 2020. | Delays in bank statements. 

The LGU has yet to enroll in the LBP “We Access” 

Reiterated in CY 2019 AAR Observation No. 1

The submitted RPCPPE by the LGU does not follow the standard/ required format of the report because of the absence of the itemized quantity per ledger and no comparison of per count and per ledger showing the difference in quantity and amount. | Reiterated in CY 2019 AAR Observation No. 2

Municipal Accountant provided depreciation for some PPE accounts but the supporting schedules were not submitted to the Auditor for verification. | Reiterated in CY 2019 AAR Observation No. 2

Not all road networks were identified with description and cost segregation of road components. 

Not all road networks have ledgers particularly those that were implemented prior to PPSAS such as ADB- INFRES.funded FMR  projects. | Reiterated in CY 2019 AAR Observation No. 2

No accumulated depreciation for road networks is reported in the Notes to FS and impairment losses were not provided. | Reiterated in CY 2019 AAR Observation No. 2

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management/ Local Chief Executive:
Observe strictly the provisions of IRR of RA 9184 on the timely preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), duly approved by the LCE/ HoPE, as basis in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget and in the disbursement of funds for the acquisition of goods and services/ infrastructure projects.

2.Require all heads of offices to submit their PPMPs for the ensuing year to be incorporated/ consolidated in the APP by the BAC Secretariat.

3.Municipal Budget Officer and MPD Officer to have a budget plan/ scheduling to be observed by departments/ office units in the preparation of their PPMPs  to be incorporated in the APP and the Annual Investment Plan; and

Whenever practicable, consolidate purchase requests for commonly used office supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to enable the Municipality to avail of substantial discounts.

We recommended that Management: 

Provide adequate budget for the enhancement of the ECPAC accounting system that is capable of preparation/ generation of the following reports:

a. The Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) in accordance with Section 18 of the NGAS Manual; 

b. The Statement of Comparison of Budgeted and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) that can be verified based on supporting documents and records in compliance to Paragraph 27 of PPSAS No. 1 and Section 111 of PD 1445 to ensure that the budgeted and actual amounts of income, expenditure and capital outlay accounts reported in the SCBAA are fairly presented.

Require the Municipal Budget Office to maintain accurate subsidiary records in posting the actual obligations by using the details of the Disbursement Voucher as supporting documents instead of the Purchase Request which may not be actualized or realized as an expenditure or obligation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office to communicate with the system’s developer for system reprogramming that would enable the Municipality to classify consumers and apply the correct water rates in accordance with its Revenue Code.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Economic Enterprise Development Office (MEEDO) to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected water system fees based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
b. based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Office to recognize Accounts Receivable and Waterworks System Fees
.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected rent income based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Office to recognize Accounts Receivable and Receipts from Market Operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe COA Circular No. 96-11 by prioritizing the preparation and the timely submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements to the COA Auditor and to enroll in the on-line facility of the Banks for viewing and printing access of bank statements to facilitate preparation thereof.

Municipal Accountant provided depreciation for some PPE accounts but the supporting schedules were not submitted to the Auditor for verification.

observations.reason: The required disclosures in the Notes to FS has not been submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: b. Issue demand letters to the NGAs and NGOs/POs to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.reason: Could no longer locate the records

observations.recommendation: d. Submit the annual Accomplishment Report/ utilization report of the budget allocated for the activities of the MCPC, copy furnished the COA Auditor.
observations.reason: No RAAOs prepared by Accounting and improper recording of Obligation by the Budget Office using Purchase Request as source document

observations.recommendation: a. Strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual;
observations.reason: Segregation of disbursement vouchers per fund and labeling is done by only one person

observations.recommendation: Likewise, turn over the receipts and disbursement records with all paid vouchers and journals evidencing the transaction to the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding financial statements for each local economic enterprise so that top management and stakeholders are properly guided in their decision-making.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and training in the use of ECPAC accounting system.

observations.recommendation: b. Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to revert/transfer back to the General Fund, the unexpended DRRM – Capital Outlay portion erroneously transferred to the Trust Liabilities – DRMM under the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Mis-looked by the Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observe COA Circular No. 96-11 by prioritizing the preparation and the timely submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements to the COA Auditor and to enroll in the on-line facility of the Banks for viewing and printing access of bank statements to facilitate preparation thereof.

We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the General Services Officer-Designate,  strictly observe the proper management and accounting of supplies and inventory in accordance with Sections 114, 115 and 124 of the NGAS Manual by:
Recognizing an inventory account upon delivery of procured supplies and materials (Section 114);
Maintaining supply/materials stock card and ledger cards under the perpetual inventory method (Section 115); and
Creating an inventory committee who will be responsible in the conduct of physical count of supplies every semester, and furnish reports to Accounting Office for the accrual or recognition of expense and proper valuation of the unused office supplies which will be considered as inventory (Section 124).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and/ or the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer  to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor a Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 to determine the propriety in the disbursements of the Fund.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balance of the DRMM Funds from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management/ Local Chief Executive:
Observe strictly the provisions of IRR of RA 9184 on the timely preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), duly approved by the LCE/HoPE, as basis in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget and in the disbursement of funds for the acquisition of goods and services/ infrastructure projects.
Require all heads of offices to submit their PPMPs for the ensuing year to be incorporated/ consolidated in the APP by the BAC Secretariat;
Municipal Budget Officer and MPD Officer to have a budget plan/ scheduling to be observed by departments/ office units in the preparation of their PPMPs  to be incorporated in the APP and the Annual Investment Plan; and
Whenever practicable, consolidate purchase requests for commonly used office supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to enable the Municipality to avail of substantial discounts.

We recommended that Management strictly observe Administrative Order No. 17 s. 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 to comply with the requirements of the DBM-PS in the preparation and submission of the APP-CSE to ensure inclusion of the Municipality’s inventory requirements in the government-wide projected common-use supplies and equipment procurement in the fiscal year.

We recommended that Management:
Provide adequate budget for the enhancement of the ECPAC accounting system that is capable of preparation/ generation of the following reports:
The Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) in accordance with Section 18 of the NGAS Manual;
The Statement of Comparison of Budgeted and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) that can be verified based on supporting documents and records in compliance to Paragraph 27 of PPSAS No. 1 and Section 111 of PD 1445 to ensure that the budgeted and actual amounts of income, expenditure and capital outlay accounts reported in the SCBAA are fairly presented.
Require the Municipal Budget Office to maintain accurate subsidiary records in posting the actual obligations by using the details of the Disbursement Voucher as supporting documents instead of the Purchase Request which may not be actualized or realized as an expenditure or obligation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office to communicate with the system’s developer for system reprogramming that would enable the Municipality to classify consumers and apply the correct water rates in accordance with its Revenue Code.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Economic Enterprise Development Office (MEEDO) to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected water system fees based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Office to recognize Accounts Receivable and Waterworks System Fees.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected rent income based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Office to recognize Accounts Receivable and Receipts from Market Operations.
Require the OIC-GSO or the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit to the Auditor  the  Report on Physical Count of PPEs (RPCPPE) by type, reconciled with accounting and property records;
observations.reason: Started in January 2018 but not yet completed because of lack of personnel. OIC-GSO was on leave for several months.

observations.recommendation: Submit the Registry of Public Infrastructure (RPI) to validate the recording of the de-recognized assets prior to the effectivity of the PPSAS;
observations.reason: Management did not prepare/ maintain RPI.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule for depreciable assets;
observations.reason: Very tedious,  particularly for assets with no available records or property ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: e) Require the Accounting Office and the GSO to prepare and maintain the prescribed records in accordance with Sections 13, 15, 16, 45 and 57 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office now maintains PPE ledger cards for newly acquired assets, but not in the prescribed format per NGAS Manual.

observations.recommendation: a. Exert diligent efforts to locate the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and to prepare subsidiary ledgers of the receivable accounts.
observations.reason: No SL or schedule was maintained by Accounting for those accounts long outstanding of more than ten years amounting t0 P865,981.24. Fund Transfer pertaining to KALAHI-CIDDS project  of Brgy San Pedro was already liquidated and booked in Sept. 2018..

observations.recommendation: c. Conduct a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that the fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose.
d. Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and recognize impairment for accounts no longer collectible.
e.If collections of these accounts are no longer probable, prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

a. Require the Special Disbursing Officer to submit the Report of Disbursements and supporting documents to the Accountant as soon as the special activity is completed and to the Auditor within the prescribed timelines in accordance with Item 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 and Item 5.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: The SDOs were submitting the liquidation reports to the Accountant but the accountant did not submit the LRs/JEVs to the Auditor within the prescribed timelines

observations.recommendation: The Accountable Officer and the Accountant observe strictly the documentary requirements in the granting and liquidation of cash advances embodied under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and submit the required lacking documents and information/ data.
observations.reason: Except for one SDO, others were liquidating their cash advances on time and no cash advances were granted without liquidating the previous ones.

observations.recommendation: a. Fast track the submission of the Solid Waste Management Plan to the DENR for final approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission;
b. Cease the operation of the illegal open dumpsite and scout a land suitable for sanitary landfill;
c. Intensify awareness of  proper waste segregation and strict implementation of best practices that mitigates air and water pollution through passage of municipal ordinance;

d. Educate its people, particularly farmers, for the production of organic fertilizers sourced from agricultural wastes  in coordination with the  Department of Agriculture for funding and technical assistance, and
e. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitor the compliance and implementation of the provisions under RA 9003.
a. Create and establish the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC) by issuing an Office Order copy furnished the COA Auditor;

b. Enjoin its MCPC to actively participate in the planning, implementation and monitoring of its projects, programs and activities to identify and resolve issues and problems on children in their locality;
c. Observe the activities to be performed by the MCPC as provided under Item B of DILG Memorandum Circular 2005-007 and  Item II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120. and to
a. Municipal DRRM Officer to ensure that the planned disaster-related activities and programs are implemented within the year;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
Exert diligent efforts to locate the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and to prepare subsidiary ledgers of the receivable accounts.
Issue demand letters to the NGAs and NGOs/POs to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
Conduct a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that the fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose.
Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and recognize impairment for accounts no longer collectible.
If collections of these accounts are no longer probable, prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Special Disbursing Officer submit the Report of Disbursements and supporting documents to the Accountant as soon as the special activity is completed and to the Auditor within the prescribed timelines in accordance with Item 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 and Item 5.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer and the Accountant observe strictly the documentary requirements in the granting and liquidation of cash advances embodied under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and submit the required lacking documents and information/ data.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding financial statements for each local economic enterprise so that top management and stakeholders are properly guided in their decision-making.

observations.reason:
The management has difficulty in the conduct of physical inventory due to manpower and time constraints.
Initially, the management has started to conduct inventory by department/ office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to submit suspension and variation order, if any, for the conduct of post audit and evaluation of the infrastructure projects. 

Also, direct the Agency Head and Municipal Engineer to impose and file liquidated damages to the contractor and to strictly comply with Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

The Management instructed the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to prepare the building plan as a requisite in the application of GSIS Insurance. The Budget Officer is directed to provide allocation for the insurance of office building.

We recommended that the Agency should instruct the Inventory Committee to submit the IIRUP.  Require the Accounting Office to reclassify the obsolete, non-functional and those no longer used in the operation (as shown in the IIRUP) from their respective asset accounts to Other Assets account.  Create an Appraisal Committee to evaluate the unserviceable properties, comply with the documents required under Annex M.E. of COA Memorandum 2005-027, and recommend the applicable mode of disposal of the items in a Disposal Committee resolution for the Local Chief Executive’s approval.  Submit these documents to the Auditor for evaluation and action thereon prior to the disposal of unserviceable items.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Department prioritize the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) and adhere to the provisions of paragraph 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management monitor constantly  the implementation of the fund transfers within the time stipulated in the activity design and program of work submitted by the barangay.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management, particularly the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and BAC Technical Working Group to observe Sections 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 pertaining to the timely preparation and submission to the COA Audit Team Leader of perfected contracts and purchase orders  together with complete supporting documents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to submit suspension and variation order, if any, for the conduct of post audit and evaluation of the infrastructure projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer impose liquidated damages to the contractor and to strictly comply with Section 8.1, Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management attend trainings and seminars only those with accreditation from the Civil Service Commission.
Also, require the Officers and Employees to strictly comply with the rules and regulations pertaining to liquidation of cash advances for travel.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 and Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the building and other structures with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to strictly comply and adhere to the provisions on the preparation, submission, review and endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budget and timely submission of GAD Accomplishment Report as illustrated under Annexes of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office prepare the program of work with target dates of completion and hire additional manpower or officer so that  the Municipal Engineer can focus and closely monitor the implementation of projects as planned to fast track the implementation of the 20% Municipal Development Projects.

We recommended that Management should instruct the Municipal Accountant – Designate to provide depreciation of the property, plant and equipment for fair presentation of the financial statements and prepare adjusting journal entry to correct the account.
observations.reason:
The management has difficulty in the computation of depreciation expense due to absence of physical inventory report. 

Initially, purchases of PPE in CY2014 and CY2015 were computed with depreciation. | . Discussed during the exit conference

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant-Designate to  transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMC Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund account solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section  21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No.2012-002.

Require the LDRRMC should decide and incorporate the programs, projects and activities of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which is prepared annually.

Inquire the Local Disaster Risk Management Officer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the required reports under COA Circular N0. 2012-002, such as the LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF using the Annex B Format should be prepared and submitted to the agencies stated above for compliance.
Instructions were made to the Municipal Accountant – Designate to strictly comply with the provisions of General Appropriation Act or Republic Act No. 10651.
observations.reason: There were employees whose loans were not fully paid | . Discussed during the exit conference

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Municipal Accountant – Designate to submit the Individual Profile of Loans, Summary of Loans and its corresponding supporting documents.

We recommended that the Management and its Local School Board should strictly observed Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 on SEF utilization. It should stop providing appropriation for MOOE expenses in the Local School Board Budget which are not in accordance with the above cited law/circular, instead charge all ineligible budget items under Section 272 to the regular DepEd Budget.

We recommended to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee, BAC-Secretariat and BAC-TWG to ensure the timely preparation and submission of contracts and purchase order and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with.

We recommended to require the following:

Direct the Municipal Mayor to install and implement a strong internal control and require the Municipal Accountant – Designate to strictly comply with the complete attachment of supporting documents before payment shall be made.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant – Designate to properly record all transactions to its appropriate account code.

We recommended to require the following:

Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary registries as defined and  provided under Section 17 to 24, Volume II of the NGAS Manual. 
 
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their records on allotments available for obligation.

We recommended that Management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive designated a point person in securing water permit from the NWRB.
observations.reason: Application for water permit entails long procedures and processes | .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount withheld to the BIR pursuant to RA 8291, PD 1752 and RR No. 2-98 so that the same could be utilized by the recipient agency for the benefit of their constituents/members and to avoid penalties due to delayed remittance.

We recommended that the Management should instruct the Local Finance Committee that at least 5% of the annual appropriation be allocated for  GAD related activities as provided under RA 7192, EO 243, and DBM-NEDA-PCW Joint Circular No. 2012-01. 

Also, require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to strictly comply and adhere the provisions on the preparation, submission, review and endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budget as illustrated under Annexes A, B, & C of the attached PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor require the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways;

Use the assessment roll in setting up the targeted receivables-Real Property Tax to arrive at a more realistic and reliable amount, thereby avoiding a material amount of receivable which could be doubtful as to collectability; and

Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.
observations.reason:
The Management has exerted effort to intensify tax collection.


However, there are some issues that need to be addressed by the Provincial Government.
